rationale: The citing paper discusses Atefi et al. (2020) in dedicated theory sections on disclosure of sensitive information, not just in a background list. It uses Atefi et al.'s finding that cost transparency is more effective when proactive/early to position a key contrast: customer-centric contract changes (legitimate power relinquishment) can still improve outcomes when reactive to customer requests. The cited paper is therefore used to shape the conceptual framing and interpretive contribution of the new study.
relation_description: The citing paper refines the implications drawn from Atefi et al. (2020) by qualifying when power-relinquishing salesperson behaviors work. It contrasts proactive informational transparency effects with its own evidence that reactive legitimate-power relinquishment via contract changes can also generate positive customer outcomes.
